target: SEC-6.4: Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions — WIN-024 taxonomy fix (ISS-711) and preemptive defense paragraph (ISS-712)
replacement_html:
Roaring 40s (WIN-024) — false causal attribution: Claims the 40–50°S wind belt coincides with the "SAA southern boundary." The Roaring 40s are caused by the temperature gradient between the equator and south pole driving westerly winds with minimal continental friction — a straightforward consequence of atmospheric circulation on a rotating body. The dome claims a causal connection between the SAA boundary and the wind belt, but provides no mechanism by which geomagnetic field intensity would drive atmospheric circulation. The spatial overlap is approximate (SAA southern boundary ~30–35°S vs Roaring 40s ~40–50°S) and non-discriminating — both globe and dome models place features at southern mid-latitudes. Note: WIN-024 is categorized as false causal attribution rather than relabeling, since its code_analysis tags confirm relabels_standard=false — the dome is not renaming the standard explanation but instead asserting a spurious causal link between unrelated phenomena.

Addressing the relabeling critique: A natural objection is: "If standard physics explains the same observations, isn't that what any good theory should do? Re-explaining known phenomena isn't a flaw — it's a requirement." This is a fair point, and we acknowledge it. Reproducing known results is a minimum bar for any scientific theory. The critique here is narrower and more specific: in the cases above, the dome model does not derive the predicted values from its own geometric parameters (disc radius, firmament height, sun altitude). Instead, it imports the globe-derived numerical values — H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc, tidal constituent periods from lunar orbital mechanics, seismic shadow zone angles from core refraction models — and attaches dome-flavored labels. The relabeling critique applies when the new framework does no independent explanatory work: it cannot predict these values from first principles, it cannot explain why these values take the magnitudes they do, and it breaks if the imported globe values are removed. The {{CA_NONRELABEL}} WINs that do not relabel standard physics are analyzed individually in Part 3, where they are found to be refuted by data, self-contradicted, or unfalsifiable on their own terms. The strongest illustration is the P-wave shadow zone (WIN-064) above: the dome's proposed mechanism is not merely redundant with standard physics — it is geometrically incompatible with the azimuthal symmetry of the observed shadow zone. That is the difference between legitimate re-explanation and hollow relabeling.
holes_addressed: ISS-711: WIN-024 taxonomy mismatch — relabels_standard=false but grouped under relabeling section. Fixed by changing subheading from "related pattern, false correlation" to "false causal attribution" and adding explicit note about code_analysis tag agreement.

ISS-712: Curmudgeon defense_survives=3 for "relabeling proves too much" objection. Added full paragraph addressing: (1) relabeling critique targets hollow relabeling not legitimate re-explanation, (2) the 30% non-relabeling WINs are addressed individually in Part 3, (3) WIN-064 P-wave shadow zone prominently featured as exemplary case of geometric incompatibility vs mere redundancy.
new_evidence_added: Explicit acknowledgment that reproducing known results IS a minimum bar for theories — charitable framing before showing why it fails here
Cross-reference to Part 3 for individual analysis of non-relabeling WINs
WIN-064 as exemplar of geometric incompatibility (not just redundancy)
Added {{CA_NONRELABEL}} placeholder for computed count of non-relabeling WINs

anticipated_objections.objection: Your own tags say WIN-024 does not relabel standard physics — so why is it in the relabeling section?
anticipated_objections.response: Fixed: WIN-024 subheading now explicitly identifies it as "false causal attribution" distinct from relabeling, with a note explaining the tag agreement. It remains in SEC-6.4 as a related but distinct structural failure pattern.

anticipated_objections.objection: Any good theory should explain the same observations as standard physics — relabeling is not a criticism.
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged and addressed directly. The critique targets cases where the dome imports globe-derived numbers wholesale rather than deriving them from dome parameters. The difference between legitimate re-explanation and hollow relabeling is whether the framework does independent explanatory work.

anticipated_objections.objection: 30% of WINs do not relabel standard physics — where is the analysis of those?
anticipated_objections.response: Cross-referenced to Part 3 where each is individually analyzed and found refuted, self-contradicted, or unfalsifiable.

kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon is right that scientific theories legitimately re-explain known phenomena — this is not inherently a flaw. The critique must be precise: it targets hollow relabeling (importing values without derivation) rather than genuine theoretical unification. The dome model gets credit for attempting to unify diverse phenomena under a single framework; the failure is that the unification relies on importing rather than deriving.
